name: Outland
short_description: Outland is a fast-paced, dynamic platformer where the player uses powers of light and dark in an epic journey to save the world from forces of chaos.
detailed_description:
Outland is a fast-paced, dynamic platformer where the player uses powers of light and dark in an epic journey to save the world from forces of chaos.

In-between Balance and Chaos stands one man, whose efforts will either bridge the ancient divide or doom the world to destruction. Outland is an ambitious 2D-platformer with a beautiful visual style and a unique, polarity-switching gameplay system. Your adventure will shift between light and darkness as you struggle to adapt in an ever-changing world!
about_the_game:
Outland is a fast-paced, dynamic platformer where the player uses powers of light and dark in an epic journey to save the world from forces of chaos.

In-between Balance and Chaos stands one man, whose efforts will either bridge the ancient divide or doom the world to destruction. Outland is an ambitious 2D-platformer with a beautiful visual style and a unique, polarity-switching gameplay system. Your adventure will shift between light and darkness as you struggle to adapt in an ever-changing world!
supported_languages:
English*, French, Italian, German, Spanish - Spain
*languages with full audio support

reviews.review_text:
[h1] STORY & STORY-TELLING [/h1]
Our game takes place in a magical world where dark and light forces exist. Our character experiences a heroic story where they learn to control the power of both sides over time. In the game, red powers represent darkness, while blue powers represent light. Using both powers, our character solves puzzles, overcomes obstacles, and battles creatures. Both powers are required at times in puzzles and skill-based obstacles. For example, some enemies only take damage based on their color. So, if you want to defeat a red enemy, you need to switch to the blue power, or the “light power,” and defeat the enemy with the “dark power.” If you don't do this, forget about defeating even the simplest enemy. Additionally, there are character upgrades scattered across specific locations on the maps, and collecting these will be a huge help in the game. But if you couldn't collect them on your first try or missed some, don't worry; the game gives you the option to replay those sections and collect those upgrades :D


[h1] GAMEPLAY DETAILS [/h1]
The game's core mechanics are built around platforming elements. And I should mention that this game has a structure that can be quite challenging in terms of platforming, because you frequently encounter situations where you have to do both combat and platforming, or both puzzles and platforming, at the same time or in quick succession. This pushes you towards a structure that can challenge you in many ways and is far from monotonous.


[h1] VISUAL STYLE & QUALITY [/h1]
Our game has graphics so vibrant and beautiful that you can enjoy just watching it, even if you put the game aside. Despite being 2.5D visuals, they have managed to create a very successful graphic structure thanks to their good use of ambient lighting and drawing details. I can say that you will find it hard to resist watching the game's world and beautiful atmosphere instead of playing the game.


[h1] VERDICT [/h1]
Although the storytelling didn't seem to be given much attention, the game has a playtime of approximately 8-12 hours depending on your platform skills, and if you enjoy and love platform games, you should definitely experience this one. Because our game is a very successful structure that fully justifies its price, featuring beautiful environment depictions, a nice ambiance, a beautifully lit and drawn world, challenging but enjoyable gameplay, a reasonable level of enemy variety, and partial innovations provided by the powers in the game. It's a game that should definitely not be missed during sales. Don't miss it, get it, and experience it.


[h1] Rating [/h1]
[*]Graphics = 9
[*]Gameplay = 8
[*]Story = 8
[*]Enemy, Power Usage, and Platform Variety = 8
[*]Boss System = 9

[*]Overall Rating = 8.4

I only got this game cuz someone notified me it was free,its been a decade and I havent still played it and now I see its delisted.It might be a another decade till I play it.Glad it still has a steam store page tho,so now I can see what I was missing and forget it for another decade.
Wonderful puzzle gameplay. I greatly enjoy it. Really interested in the CO-OP option, so glad they included it!

I quit at the final boss. I really love the platforming and hate the combat, so the game goes from good to progressively worse and worse as combat becomes a bigger portion of the gameplay. I'm so frustrated and hate that I'm quitting right at the finish line but it's just not worth it and I need to take all my anger out on something.

Outland is a visually striking Metroidvania platformer that nails its core light/dark color-switching mechanic — toggling between red and blue to match or counter enemies adds a satisfying layer of complexity that evolves nicely throughout the game. The backgrounds are gorgeous, the soundtrack fits each environment well, and the boss fights are genuinely epic highlights. That said, the game does show its weaknesses: the levels start to blend together after a while, the story is forgettable, and the linear structure feels at odds with the Metroidvania label. The bullet-hell sections can get visually chaotic to the point of confusion, and keyboard controls leave something to be desired. Still, if you enjoy tight platforming with a clever gimmick that keeps building on itself, Outland is worth picking up — especially at its low price point.
